bullets.text: Eight children, aged one to 14, were killed in a shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana, which police are investigating as a domestic disturbance.

More than 
A31bn has been pledged for Sudan at a conference in Berlin, exceeding the funding target, though prospects for a ceasefire remain distant as the humanitarian crisis deepens.
The Swiss National Bank has cut its interest rates by a half point to 0.5%, with the dollar subsequently jumping against the franc.
The Middle East crisis continues with Iran reportedly rejecting US peace talks, while US President Trump claims American marines took custody of an Iranian-flagged vessel.
US President Donald Trump's unreliable diplomatic style is causing confusion in Iran talks, but Tehran remains clear on the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz.
US officials are set to travel to Pakistan for a new round of peace talks, despite Tehran's rejection of initial US peace overtures and ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

The US Navy seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman after it breached a blockade, with President Trump stating the ship's engine room was damaged and US Marines are now in custody of the vessel.

President Trump announced he would send representatives to Pakistan for peace talks aimed at ending the Iran conflict, while also reiterating threats of striking civilian infrastructure if Tehran does not agree to terms.
Economists are warning that the economic impact of the war, particularly higher fuel prices, will lead to persistent inflation in the US even after the conflict concludes.
